Over the years, the department has engaged with a large number of organizations including NAL, GTRE, HAL, DRDL, ADE, ARDE, ADA, ADRDE, ISRO, IAF, USAF, BARC, Boeing, Pratt and Whitney, Airbus and GE for scientific and engineering research. The department seeks applications from dynamic and motivated candidates with excellent academic record along with specialization in the any of the following domains: Aerodynamics stream : – Aeroacoustics; Aerodynamics of bio-inspired vehicles; Fluid-structure interactions (FSI), computational FSI; High-fidelity computational aerodynamics simulation for low/high-speed flow regimes; Testing and instrumentation for low/high-speed flows. Aerospace propulsion stream : – Turbomachinery: experimental methods, full-scale computations, and flutter; Electric propulsion; Novel propulsion systems for drones and space applications; Solid propellants. Aerospace structures stream : – Dynamics and vibrations of flexible structures; Aeroelasticity: experimental and computational methods; Experimental solid mechanics; Design of aerospace vehicle structures (including vehicles for space exploration); Structures for spacecraft and satellite applications; Structural integrity and health monitoring. Flight dynamics and controls stream : – Flight dynamics of aircraft (fixed, rotary and flapping wings), coupled computational aeromechanics and control; Satellite and spacecraft dynamics; Flight vehicle guidance and control. ads.title: Assistant Professor in Philosophy
ads.raw_text_excerpt: We are inviting applications from highly research-active candidates for the position of full-time Assistant Professor in Philosophy. The successful candidate will teach foundational undergraduate courses in Critical Reasoning and Applied Ethics in addition to elective philosophy courses which may include Introduction to Philosophy, Introduction to Western Philosophy, Ethics and the Good Life, Metaphysics, Epistemology, Existentialism, Political Philosophy, etc. The successful candidate will be a clear, engaging teacher of undergraduate students for whom they may be their first exposure to philosophy in an academic context. Courses may be either a term or a semester in length. In addition to fulfilling the teaching requirements, the candidate must actively pursue research in Philosophy and regularly publish their work. FLAME University offers a Ph.D. in Humanities, so qualifying faculty members can also serve as advisors in their area of expertise. Faculty responsibilities include but are not limited to teaching, advising students, participating in faculty colloquia, and academic administration. A Ph.D. (completed or nearing submission) is required at the time of application. Our expectation is that a successful applicant will: 1. Undertake independent high-quality research as well as work actively with existing members to build a strong interdisciplinary community of scholars who are committed to delivering cutting-edge research and innovation 2. Translate research findings into evidence-based practices, programs and policies 3. Remain committed to excellence in teaching- a core value of FLAME University- and adopt creative pedagogies that foster cultures of inclusion and diversity. To learn more about our teaching and research programs, please visit our website https://www.flame.edu.in .

ads.title: Literary and Cultural Studies, especially those
ads.raw_text_excerpt: We welcome applications from all subfields of Literary and Cultural Studies, especially those candidates who have a marked focus on India. The instructor will offer undergraduate courses to support the major in Literary and Cultural Studies offered in the Department of Humanities and Languages. Candidates should be prepared to teach a broad undergraduate survey course as well as a variety of elective offerings. Specialists in Literature (across regions, genres and periods for Survey of Literature offerings), Critical Theory (across Continental, Contemporary, Gender and Film Studies) and Cultural Studies in India are all invited to apply. Degrees in related fields, including but not limited to cultural anthropology, sociology, and/or area studies, will also be considered. ads.title: Visiting Faculty Positions (Open Rank, 2026–2027)
ads.raw_text_excerpt: Ashoka University, Department of Sociology & Anthropology — Visiting Faculty Positions (Open Rank | 2026–2027). Sonipat, Haryana. Open: 1 May 2026. Deadline: 1 Jun 2026 (11:59 PM ET). Semesters: Monsoon (25 Aug – 13 Dec 2026), Spring (19 Jan – 9 May 2027). Apply for one or both semesters. Preferred areas: STS, Gender & Sexuality, Caste, Legal Anthropology, Medical Anthropology. Qualifications: PhD; strong teaching; active research. Responsibilities: Teach 2 courses/semester; engage in academic activities. Apply with: Cover Letter, CV, Syllabi, Teaching Statement.
